text: The patient is positioned supine with a 30 degree lateral decubitus.
The incision of the mini thoracotomy is usually performed at the level of the fourth or fifth intercostal space, depending on the chest structure and the heart orientation on the x-ray.
The routine trochors arrangement is achieved by placing one five millimeter trochor at the level of the third or fourth intercostal space, and the other five millimeter trochors
at the 6 or 7 intercostal space on the medial posterior axillary line.
Such trochers will be utilized for an endoscopic grasper and the harmonius capo.
The 30 degree endoscopic camera is introduced, CO2 insufflation started and the lima visually inspected.
The remaining trochers are placed as previously outlined.
The lima preparation as a pedicle is started, first by dissecting the medial side of the endothoracic fascia.
Then, also the lateral side of the endothoracic fascia is divided.
Once the fascia has been dissected free all along the lima course, division of side branches is started.
A gentle spatulation allows for side branches identification.
The division of side branches can be safely accomplished by using the ammonia scalpel.
With the endoscopic technique, the lima can be harvested proximally up to its origin from the subclavian artery.
This limit of lima harvesting is usually provided by assessing the position of the apex of the heart.
Once lima harvesting has been completed, the coronary anastomosis is performed by a mini thoracotomy.
